channel.title: dotnet/roslyn Issues
channel.description: Issues from https://github.com/dotnet/roslyn, generated by https://github.com/meziantou/IssuesToRss/

item.title: Issue: Constant evaluation related to Float-> Int conversion are somehow broken in Roslyn - @deniszykov
item.description:
Description

Prior to unknown version of CS compiler this expression evaluates to 24:

const byte myConst = unchecked((byte)(float)-1000);
> myConst 
0

Now it evaluates to 0.

Mono/old .NET Framework compiler evaluates it to 24. If you do this conversion in runtime like this:

var x = (float)-1000;
> (byte)x
24

You get a right result. So something is broken in constant evaluation for Roslyn compiler.

Configuration

  • .NET Framework version: 4.0.30319.42000
  • dotnet version -> SDK Version: 5.0.302
  • csc -version -> 3.10.0-4.21329.37 (246ce641)
  • Windows 10 x64

Regression?

Yes, but I don't know which version did broke it.

item.title: Issue: Error deserializing SerializableOptionSet on ServiceHub Core Host - @genlu
item.description:
Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Enable servicehub core host in VS 
    <VSInstallPath>\Common7\IDE\VsRegEdit.exe set <VSInstallPath> RoslynDev HKCU "Roslyn\Internal\OnOff\Features" OOPCoreClr dword 1
  2. Open Roslyn.sln in VS
  3. You will see goldbar showing errors like this:
07/20/2021 11:09:57 Pacific Standard Time: Error : 1 :[ServiceHub.RoslynCodeAnalysisService:19964] Unexpected exception: System.InvalidOperationException: Unexpected value 'Type' of type 'Roslyn.Utilities.ObjectWriter+EncodingKind'
   at Roslyn.Utilities.ObjectReader.ReadStringValue(EncodingKind kind) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6000178+0x74
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Options.SerializableOptionSet.<Deserialize>g__TryDeserializeOptionKey|15_1(ObjectReader reader, ILookup`2 lookup, OptionKey& deserializedOptionKey) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6001742+0x0
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Options.SerializableOptionSet.Deserialize(ObjectReader reader, IOptionService optionService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6001740+0x81
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Serialization.SerializerService.DeserializeOptionSet(ObjectReader reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6001531+0x13
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Serialization.SerializerService.Deserialize[T](WellKnownSynchronizationKind kind, ObjectReader reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Workspaces.dll:token 0x600151d+0x15e
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteHostAssetSerialization.ReadData(Stream stream, Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6000088+0x6a
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteHostAssetSerialization.ReadDataAsync(PipeReader pipeReader, Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6000087+0x87
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteHostAssetSerialization.ReadDataAsync(PipeReader pipeReader, Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6000087+0x182
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.BrokeredServiceConnection`1.<>c__DisplayClass21_0`1.<<InvokeStreamingServiceAsync>b__1>d.MoveNext() in Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.Workspaces.dll:token 0x6000205+0xaf

item.title: Issue: "Unsupported encoding" error in Servicehub core host - @genlu
item.description:
Steps to Reproduce:

1 . Enable servicehub core host in VS

<VSInstallPath>\Common7\IDE\VsRegEdit.exe set <VSInstallPath> <Hive> HKCU "Roslyn\Internal\OnOff\Features" OOPCoreClr dword 1

  1. create a new empty class in a project

  2. "file -> save as" the new class

  3. choose to save with encoding in the dialog

  4. pick codepage 1252

  5. close and restart vs with the same project

  6. you should see a bunch of infobars about encoding errors

Expected Behavior:

No errors in OOP

Actual Behavior:

Goldbar showing a bunch of features hit the same error, for example:

StreamJsonRpc.RemoteInvocationException: 'Windows-1252' is not a supported encoding name. For information on defining a custom encoding, see the documentation for the Encoding.RegisterProvider method. (Parameter 'name')
   at StreamJsonRpc.JsonRpc.<InvokeCoreAsync>d__139`1.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task task)
   at System.Threading.Tasks.ValueTask`1.get_Result()
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.BrokeredServiceConnection`1.<TryInvokeAsync>d__17`1.MoveNext() in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\BrokeredServiceConnection.cs:line 200
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at Microsoft.VisualStudio.Telemetry.WindowsErrorReporting.WatsonReport.GetClrWatsonExceptionInfo(Exception exceptionObject)
RPC server exception:
System.ArgumentException: 'Windows-1252' is not a supported encoding name. For information on defining a custom encoding, see the documentation for the Encoding.RegisterProvider method. (Parameter 'name')
      at System.Text.EncodingTable.InternalGetCodePageFromName(String name) in System.Private.CoreLib.dll:token 0x6003234+0xba
      at System.Text.EncodingTable.GetCodePageFromName(String name) in System.Private.CoreLib.dll:token 0x6003233+0x24
      at System.Text.Encoding.GetEncoding(String name) in System.Private.CoreLib.dll:token 0x60031ae+0x0
      at Roslyn.Utilities.ObjectReader.ReadValueWorker() in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Compilers\Core\Portable\Serialization\ObjectReader.cs:line 284
      at Roslyn.Utilities.ObjectReader.ReadValue() in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Compilers\Core\Portable\Serialization\ObjectReader.cs:line 207
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Serialization.SerializerService.DeserializeSerializableSourceText(ObjectReader reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Core\Portable\Serialization\SerializerService_Asset.cs:line 53
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Serialization.SerializerService.Deserialize[T](WellKnownSynchronizationKind kind, ObjectReader reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Core\Portable\Serialization\SerializerService.cs:line 215
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteHostAssetSerialization.ReadData(Stream stream, Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\RemoteHostAssetSerialization.cs:line 191
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteHostAssetSerialization.ReadDataAsync(PipeReader pipeReader, Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\RemoteHostAssetSerialization.cs:line 130
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteHostAssetSerialization.ReadDataAsync(PipeReader pipeReader, Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\RemoteHostAssetSerialization.cs:line 143
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.BrokeredServiceConnection`1.<>c__DisplayClass21_0`1.<<InvokeStreamingServiceAsync>b__1>d.MoveNext() in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\BrokeredServiceConnection.cs:line 325
   --- End of stack trace from previous location ---
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.BrokeredServiceConnection`1.<>c__DisplayClass21_0`1.<<InvokeStreamingServiceAsync>b__1>d.MoveNext() in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\BrokeredServiceConnection.cs:line 334
   --- End of stack trace from previous location ---
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.BrokeredServiceConnection`1.InvokeStreamingServiceAsync[TResult](TService service, Func`4 invocation, Func`3 reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\BrokeredServiceConnection.cs:line 337
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteCallback`1.InvokeAsync[TResult](Func`4 invocation, Func`3 reader, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\Core\RemoteCallback.cs:line 68
      at Microsoft.VisualStudio.Telemetry.WindowsErrorReporting.WatsonReport.GetClrWatsonExceptionInfo(Exception exceptionObject) in Microsoft.VisualStudio.Telemetry.dll:token 0x6000993+0x2
   --- End of stack trace from previous location ---
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.SolutionAssetSource.GetAssetsAsync(Int32 scopeId, ISet`1 checksums, ISerializerService serializerService, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\SolutionAssetSource.cs:line 34
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.AssetProvider.RequestAssetsAsync(ISet`1 checksums, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\AssetProvider.cs:line 151
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.AssetProvider.SynchronizeAssetsAsync(ISet`1 checksums, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\AssetProvider.cs:line 122
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.ChecksumSynchronizer.SynchronizeProjectAssets_NoLockAsync(IEnumerable`1 projectChecksums, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\ChecksumSynchronizer.cs:line 75
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.ChecksumSynchronizer.SynchronizeSolutionAssetsAsync(Checksum solutionChecksum, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\ChecksumSynchronizer.cs:line 45
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.AssetProvider.SynchronizeSolutionAssetsAsync(Checksum solutionChecksum, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\AssetProvider.cs:line 85
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteWorkspace.CreateSolution_NoLockAsync(AssetProvider assetProvider, Checksum solutionChecksum, Boolean fromPrimaryBranch, Int32 workspaceVersion, Solution baseSolution, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\RemoteWorkspace.cs:line 148
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteWorkspace.GetSolutionAsync(AssetProvider assetProvider, Checksum solutionChecksum, Boolean fromPrimaryBranch, Int32 workspaceVersion, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Host\RemoteWorkspace.cs:line 213
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.RemoteNavigationBarItemService.<>c__DisplayClass2_0.<<GetItemsAsync>b__0>d.MoveNext() in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Services\NavigationBar\RemoteNavigationBarItemService.cs:line 32
   --- End of stack trace from previous location ---
      at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Remote.BrokeredServiceBase.RunServiceImplAsync[T](Func`2 implementation, CancellationToken cancellationToken) in C:\Users\gel\roslyn2\src\Workspaces\Remote\ServiceHub\Services\BrokeredServiceBase.cs:line 73

item.title: Issue: Reloadable types: updated versions are not callable from another assembly - @tmat
item.description:
The compiler does not have enough information to emit the right TypeRefs to new versions of reloadable types.

Repro:

App.exe:

class Program
{
    static void G()
    {
        var c = new LibClass();
        // Console.WriteLine(c.F()); // uncomment during Hot Reload session
    }

    static void Main(string[] args)
    {
        while (true)
        {
            Thread.Sleep(500);
            G();
        }
    }
}

Lib.dll referenced by App.exe

using System.Runtime.CompilerServices;

namespace System.Runtime.CompilerServices
{
    [AttributeUsage(AttributeTargets.Class | AttributeTargets.Struct)]
    internal class CreateNewOnMetadataUpdateAttribute : Attribute
    {
    }
}

[CreateNewOnMetadataUpdate]
public class LibClass
{
     // uncomment during Hot Reload session
     // public int F() => 1;
}

Run App.exe, uncomment the commented code and apply changes.

Depends on https://github.com/dotnet/runtime/issues/56037

item.title: Issue: [Bug] Syntactically incorrect "auto-fix" for IDE0038 - @deeprobin
item.description:
Version Used: .NET 5.0.102

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Create a LINQ Query with "is" and casting
  2. Hover over the where-clause
        // Example
        public IEnumerable<IApiModel> ApplyGroup(IEnumerable<IApiModel> input)
        {
            return ApplyGroupByObjects(from model in input
                                       where model is ApiObjectModel
                                       select ((ApiObjectModel)model).Object);
        }

Expected Behavior: Do not recommend "auto-fix".

Actual Behavior: I get a auto-fix recommendation, which is syntactically wrong.

item.title: Issue: Create a way to wait for updates in Worksapce for HotReload functionality - @vritant24
item.description:
As part of hot reload, Workspace.CurrentSoluton is passed on to the HotReloadService to retrieve deltas. In VS, there is a possibility of a race if the user makes an edit and immediately kicks off an operation that passes an un-updated CurrentSolution to the HotReloadService, thus skipping changes. In test experience scenarios this is not desirable.

We need a way to ask for CurrentSoluton after processing all the updates in the editor "so far".

item.title: Issue: Switch expression pattern is not using suggestion mode - @333fred
item.description:
Version Used: Version 17.0.0 Preview 3.0 [31519.82.main] (I think this is a recent break)

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Create a new console app.
  2. Type _ = args switch { $$ } (cursor at $$)
  3. Type _<space>

Expected Behavior:

_ is kept.

Actual Behavior:

#if is inserted (or whatever is top of your completion list). Here's a different example:

item.title: Issue: Verbatim identifiers in xml doc parameter tags should not generate CS1572 warning - @rdurell
item.description:
Version Used: Microsoft (R) Visual C# Compiler version 4.0.0-2.21362.3 (5f7ddf6f)

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Create a method with a verbatim identifier as a parameter name such as public void Foo(string @object)
  2. Add XML documentation with a matching parameter tag such as /// <param name="@object">Object to foo</param>
  3. Compile

Expected Behavior: The code compiles without a CS1572 (WRN_UnmatchedParamTag) warning.

Actual Behavior: A CS1572 (WRN_UnmatchedParamTag) warning is generated.

Workaround Remove the verbatim identifier "@" from the XML documentation parameter tag name attribute.

Unfortunately, the workaround is not feasible with code generated by third party code generation tools.

item.title: Issue: Detect `if (x == null) x = ...` and offer to convert to `x ??= ...` - @CyrusNajmabadi
item.description:
This should also match things like:

if (a && b && c == null)
   c = ...

item.title: Issue: Incorrect nullability inference in ternary operator with use of local variable - @kindermannhubert
item.description:
Version Used: VS 17.0.0 Preview 2.0

Steps to Reproduce:

Methods M1/2/3 are equivalent, but in the last one warning is reported.

#nullable enable
using System.Diagnostics.CodeAnalysis;
public class C 
{
    static int M1() 
    {
        if (TryGet(out var obj))
        {
            return obj.GetHashCode(); //ok
        }
        else
        {
            return -1;
        }
    }
    
    static int M2() 
    {
        return TryGet(out var obj) ? obj.GetHashCode() : -1; //ok
    }
    
    static int M3() 
    {
        var ok = TryGet(out var obj);
        return ok ? obj.GetHashCode() : -1; //warning CS8602: Dereference of a possibly null reference.
    }
    
    static bool TryGet([NotNullWhen(true)]out object? obj)
    {
        obj = new();
        return true;
    }   
}

item.title: Issue: BUG: IDE0051 not working when file contains `nameof()` - @LukeOwlclaw
item.description:
Version Used: VS2019 16.10.3 C:\Program Files\dotnet\sdk\5.0.302\Sdks\Microsoft.NET.Sdk\targets..\analyzers\Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.CSharp.NetAnalyzers.dll

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Create a new project (e.g. .NET Core 5 Console app)
  2. Add private int mTest = 5; to Program.cs

You'll see immediately: Message IDE0051 Private member 'Program.mTest' is unused

  1. Add to Main(): Console.WriteLine(nameof(Class1));

Actual Behavior:

Message IDE0051 is gone!

Expected Behavior:

Message IDE0051 should stay independent of nameof being used in file.

Screenshots: (for visualizing I set IDE0051 to "error" and used extension "Solution Error Visualizer"):

Same problem applies to IDE0052

item.title: Issue: [LSP] workspace diagnostics fail when we encounter documents in the TypeScript misc project - @dibarbet
item.description:
In razor scenarios, Typescript adds typescript files (e.g. lib.es6.d.ts) and the top level generated razor file (e.g. Counter.razor) to a 'misc project' in the main VS workspace roslyn solution.

Workspace diagnostics happily returns no diagnostics for these TS + "Counter.razor" docs. This actually doesn't cost too much computation since these files never get updated diagnostics.

This becomes a problem though as the top level razor documents (e.g. "Counter.razor") are somehow getting added to the C# project's _filePathToDocumentIds map (in the map there is a documentId pointing to "Counter.razor" that points to the project Id of the C# project) but there is no matching documentId in the same c# project in the solution. https://sourceroslyn.io/#Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Workspaces/Workspace/Solution/SolutionState.cs,44 ?

THis means that when we attempt to read the previous results, we throw since we find documents with an Id by file path (_filePathToDocumentIds contains two documentIds, one in misc TS, one in C#), but then fail to look up the document by Id since the C# project does not contain the doc. https://sourceroslyn.io/#Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Protocol/Extensions/Extensions.cs,34 Manifested in the logs as

Exception: System.InvalidOperationException: Error handling 'workspace/_ms_diagnostic' request: The solution does not contain the specified document. ---> System.InvalidOperationException: The solution does not contain the specified document.
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.Shared.Extensions.ISolutionExtensions.GetRequiredDocument(Solution solution, DocumentId documentId)
   at System.Collections.Immutable.ImmutableArray.CreateRange[TSource,TResult](ImmutableArray`1 items, Func`2 selector)
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Extensions.GetDocuments(Solution solution, Uri documentUri, String clientName)
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Handler.Diagnostics.AbstractPullDiagnosticHandler`2.HandleRemovedDocuments(RequestContext context, DiagnosticParams[] previousResults, BufferedProgress`1 progress)
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Handler.Diagnostics.AbstractPullDiagnosticHandler`2.<HandleRequestAsync>d__21.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task task)
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Handler.RequestExecutionQueue.<>c__DisplayClass15_0`2.<<ExecuteAsync>b__0>d.MoveNext()
   --- End of inner exception stack trace ---
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Handler.RequestExecutionQueue.<>c__DisplayClass15_0`2.<<ExecuteAsync>b__0>d.MoveNext()
--- End of stack trace from previous location where exception was thrown ---
   at System.Runtime.ExceptionServices.ExceptionDispatchInfo.Throw()
   at System.Runtime.CompilerServices.TaskAwaiter.HandleNonSuccessAndDebuggerNotification(Task task)
   at Microsoft.CodeAnalysis.LanguageServer.Handler.RequestExecutionQueue.QueueItem.<CallbackAsync>d__12.MoveNext()

I think there are two issues

  1. We should only compute workspace diagnostics for files with a matching client name for the server (the top level razor doc has client name "TypeScript". However the other TS files do not have this name (@uniqueiniquity should the other TS files also have the TS client name?).
  2. Something somewhere is causing a document in the C# project for razor top level documents to be created (any ideas @uniqueiniquity ?). This is then removed? from the workspace but the file path mapping lingers.

item.title: Issue: All edits in a VSIX project are marked as "Rude Edits" - @jmarolf
item.description:
1. Open Roslyn.sln
  2. Press F5 to begin building and debugging (I am using VS 2022 so I used the main-vs-deps branch)
  3. While Debugging edit any code file

Expected

edits that small and in-scope can be applied without issues (such as adding and if statement)

Actual

All edits are treated as rude edits.

item.title: Issue: Please provide a "Developer Mode" toggle that makes code analyzer cache invalidated every build - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

It's very annoying having to restart Visual Studio every time I make changes to an analyzer or source generator. Please provide a "Developer Mode" toggle that invalidates analyzer cache every build when enabled.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 5/9/2021, 11:14 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Add quick action accelerator keys - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Accelerator keys appear on drop-down menus - you can get them on the main menus by pressing alt, then various menu items get a little underscore for the key you can press to activate that menu item. Example of existing accelerators in VS:

! [accel.png] (https://aka.ms/dc/image?name=Ba9c11186793846cc8567b284515ca234637574438043718422_accel.png&tid=a9c11186793846cc8567b284515ca234637574438043718422)

So pressing Alt, D, l in that order would click the "Relaunch Performance Profiler" menu item.

The the quick code actions dropdown menu has no such accelerators, and it can end up with many refactorings/fixes especially when we take full advantage of roslyn, for example:

! [quick ac.png] (https://aka.ms/dc/image?name=B5ff1891c69ac46e2a6d554ae33fb01c4637574439246537290_quick_ac.png&tid=5ff1891c69ac46e2a6d554ae33fb01c4637574439246537290)

I don't believe there is a way to turn on accelerator keys for this menu - please correct me if there is a way.

Using this menu using the mouse/arrow keys is very cumbersome, because it can get very full as mentioned.

Please can we add accelerator keys to this menu. The menu is dynamic and can have actions coming from user defined code-fixes (e.g. roslynator), so I think the accelerators should be auto-generated e.g. the first unused key for unquoted word first-letters (that's a fast nanoseconds algorithm, can be run on menu opening without harm).

Thanks!

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 5/25/2021, 01:18 AM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Tool to help locating C# global code causing CS7022 for the real Main() - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Problem: Accidental global c# 9 code can take over from main() and is nearly impossible to find.

With the new c# global code feature, any extra semicolon or pair of brackets {} outside of a name space will take over from Program { Main() {}} and become the new main method. This will generate a CS7022 warning on the Main() method, which is good, but it is VERY difficult to find the global code that has taken over! The code will compile and run and do absolutely nothing if there is an extra semicolon or {}. Try dropping a pair of {} after the include statements but before the namespace in any file in a working application and just try and find the problem! The more cs files you have, the harder the job is to find the problem.

There should be some way to search or navigate to the code that is acting as Main() for the application. I would suggest that the CS7022 warning have a hint to take you to the real main code. Maybe if main() worked like an overrides, the navigation could behave like class hierarchy navigation.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/21/2021, 07:52 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: New quick action: Move code to partial class file - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Add a new quick action to move selected code to partial class file(Create new, or move to a exsited file)

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 7/7/2021, 07:38 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: CodeLens compact mode for reference counts - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Hello

I like the CodeLens reference count feature but i would like it to be more compact rather than forcing my code to be more spaced apart for each line which is not nice to read and is quite annoying.

I propose a simpler compact design. Here are two options provided.

Option 1

! option1. PNG

Option 2 is to put them on the line counts (this solves the issue of situations where the function has not been indented).

! [option 2.PNG] (https://aka.ms/dc/image?name=Bb9a8d8f407de471d8cbde75e74219c58637613003119386170_option_2.PNG&tid=b9a8d8f407de471d8cbde75e74219c58637613003119386170)

User's can be given the option by having a compact button:

! lens. PNG

Thanks for reading. Dave

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 7/7/2021, 07:27 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Cannot load Microsoft.CodeAnalyzers - @dansiegel
item.description:
Version Used: 3.11.0-3-final

Steps to Reproduce:

Note that I only began having these issues after updating.

I currently have .NET 6 preview 6 installed and am running the latest releases of VS stable/preview & VS 2022

  1. Create a Generator
public class MyGenerator : ISourceGenerator
{
    public void Execute(GeneratorExecutionContext context)
    {
        Debugger.Launch();
    }
}
  1. Reference the Generator Project via a ProjectReference from another project in the same solution.
<ItemGroup>
  <ProjectReference Include="..\MyGeneratorProject\MyGeneratorProject.csproj"
                    OutputItemType="Analyzer" 
                    ReferenceOutputAssembly="false" />
</ItemGroup>
  1. Build the other project

Expected Behavior:

Generator from local Analyzer project should generate code in another project where it is referenced with a ProjectReference.

Actual Behavior:

No source code is generated. A build warning shows the Generator could not be created.

An instance of the analyzer MyProject.MyGenerator cannot be created from {path to dll}: Could not load file or assembly 'Microsoft.CodeAnalysis, Version=3.11.0.0, Culture=neutral, PublicKeyToken=31b3856ad364e35' or one of its dependencies. The system cannot find the file specified.

item.title: Issue: EnC: Adding a type in a new namespace isn't handled correctly in symbol matcher - @tmat
item.description:
Tracks TODO in CSharpSymbolMatcher.VisitNamespace and VisualBasicSymbolMatcher.VisitNamespace.

Currently adding a namespace is a rude edit so this does not affect existing scenarios, but needs to be fixed to remove this rude edit.

item.title: Issue: Throw better exception when multiple semantic edits of the same symbol are passed to Compilation.EmitDifference - @tmat
Issue: Display exception explanations in tooltips - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Xml comments on C# methods in visual studio support listing the various exceptions that can occur from this function, allowing you to also input explanation of what that error is inside the exception element. This is great, except these exception comments do not display in the tooltip of function, only the exception type. I think it would be a great enhancement to display the exception comment within the tooltip.

! [image.png] (https://aka.ms/dc/image?name=B55d30304e718417890abb4c2620166e9637600082210326991_20210623-112339-image.png&tid=55d30304e718417890abb4c2620166e9637600082210326991)

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/22/2021, 07:31 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Remove unused methodes via CodeCleaner - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Would love to remove unused methodes via the CodeCleaner, especially for C#. Since we can remove unused fields and propertys, removing dead methodes would be an awesome addition.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/22/2021, 07:31 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: VB.net collapsible comment blocks inside procedure - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

would love to have the comment blocks to be collapsible when inside a function/sub, currently comment blocks are only collapsible if they are outside a function/sub.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/20/2021, 07:55 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Make intellisense provide suggestions for generic lists where <T> is the class inherited. - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Please restrict intellisense to types allowed for a generic type parameter. The entry will scroll down a list of all types that contains the class inherited. Let's say:

public T AddComponent<T>() where T : Component, new()
        {
            var component = new T();
            component. Entity = this;
            Components.Add(component);
            component. Initialize();
            return component;
        }

So all the classes that are inherited from Component Example: Public Class Ninja: Component Will be displayed whenever i use: AddComponent (< -Intellisense display of options where it suggests the classes that inherits the Component Class- >) So it will appears: AddComponent (<Ninja>)

Thanks in advance, Luke.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/20/2021, 07:55 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Copy method summary for abstract methods (c#) - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

When implementing an abstract method, the summary comments of the abstract method should be automatically added above the newly created implementing method.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/20/2021, 07:55 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Default XML comment for overriden properties/methods should be inherited. - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Add option to set default generated XML comments for inherited members as

/// <inheritdoc />

instead of

/// <summary>
/// 
/// </summary>

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/20/2021, 06:57 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Add option to disable graphical .editorconfig UI - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

I like to have full control about every single line of code that I type. The current .editorconfig UI is confusing because I see many properties that are not even listed in .editorconfig file.

Let's say that I have this .editorconfig in my solution:

[*]
indent_style = space

[*.cs]
indent_size = 4
insert_final_newline = true
charset = utf-8-bom

# IDE0005: Using directive is unnecessary.
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0005.severity = warning

# IDE0052: Remove unread private members
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0052.severity = warning

# IDE0055: Fix formatting
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0055.severity = warning

# IDE0060: Remove unused parameter
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0060.severity = warning

Then I open .editorconfig in Visual Studio and I see an absolute nonsense like "Place open braces on new lines for control blocks" which are not even mentioned in whole .editorconfig file and which I DON'T care about. Please implement either of these options:

  1. Add option do disable graphical .editorconfig UI
  2. Fix graphical .editorconfig UI so it's clear which rules are actually enforced

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/20/2021, 06:57 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

Collin Stevens on 6/21/2021, 02:47 PM:

This UI is atrocious. I have to open a new editor to edit this file now. I can’t believe this didn’t go through a preview feature flag first, it’s so horrible.

1234 on 7/14/2021, 08:36 AM:

Consider replacing the GUI with .editorconfig intellisense that allows users to explore options via type-ahead. It solves option overload and not being able to see what’s changed.

item.title: Issue: Add option to disable graphical .editorconfig UI - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

I like to have full control about every single line of code that I type. The current .editorconfig UI is confusing because I see many properties that are not even listed in .editorconfig file.

Let's say that I have this .editorconfig in my solution:

[*]
indent_style = space

[*.cs]
indent_size = 4
insert_final_newline = true
charset = utf-8-bom

# IDE0005: Using directive is unnecessary.
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0005.severity = warning

# IDE0052: Remove unread private members
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0052.severity = warning

# IDE0055: Fix formatting
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0055.severity = warning

# IDE0060: Remove unused parameter
dotnet_diagnostic. IDE0060.severity = warning

Then I open .editorconfig in Visual Studio and I see an absolute nonsense like "Place open braces on new lines for control blocks" which are not even mentioned in whole .editorconfig file and which I DON'T care about. Please implement either of these options:

  1. Add option do disable graphical .editorconfig UI
  2. Fix graphical .editorconfig UI so it's clear which rules are actually enforced

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/20/2021, 06:57 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

Collin Stevens on 6/21/2021, 02:47 PM:

This UI is atrocious. I have to open a new editor to edit this file now. I can’t believe this didn’t go through a preview feature flag first, it’s so horrible.

1234 on 7/14/2021, 08:36 AM:

Consider replacing the GUI with .editorconfig intellisense that allows users to explore options via type-ahead. It solves option overload and not being able to see what’s changed.

item.title: Issue: Expand code snippets to interact with the existing class definition so we can easily add standard features to many classes - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

If code snippets were aware of the class that they were being applied to, we could setup variables to control not only where the additional code is added but also to what.

For example, a code snippet could be created called AddFooInterface. This snippet would have a section for usings, a section for the constructor, a section to append to the class definition, and control areas to ensure that the interface was implemented in a similar area for each class.

So we could go from

using system;

public class MyFunkyClass:MyBaseClass { public MyFunkyClass() { }

public void ExistingComplexWorkThatIDoNotWantToRecreate() { } }

to

using system; using MyNamespace;

public class MyFunkyClass:MyBaseClass,IMyInterface { //IMyInterface fields private bool _thisIsCool = true;

public MyFunkyClass(bool isThisCool):base(isThisCool) { _thisIsCool = isThisCool; }

public void ExistingComplexWorkThatIDoNotWantToRecreate() { }

//IMyInterface members public bool IsThisCool() { return _thisIsCool; } }

using something like this

AddFooInterface afi Example of possible XML Chris Fricke Append

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/17/2021, 07:40 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Add Quick action to remove braces from if, for, foreach blocks - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

[severity:It bothers me. A fix would be nice] It would be nice to have some sort of quick action or hotkey that would convert single line if/for/foreach blocks into ones with braces and vice versa.

What I mean is:

A keyboard shortcut which, when the cursor is placed inside the block, would turn this:

if (true)
{
    Console.Write();
}

into this

if (true)
    Console.Write();

and vice versa.

It's annoying to have to delete the braces off such blocks when only a single instruction remains within them and vice versa when you want to add more instructions.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/14/2021, 08:17 PM:

We have directed your feedback to the appropriate engineering team for further evaluation. The team will review the feedback and notify you about the next steps.

Feedback Bot on 6/16/2021, 09:44 AM:

Thank you for sharing your feedback! Our teams prioritize action on product issues with broad customer impact. See details at: https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/visualstudio/ide/report-a-problem?view=vs-2019#faq. In case you need answers to common questions or need assisted support, be sure to use https://visualstudio.microsoft.com/vs/support/. We’ll keep you posted on any updates to this feedback.

item.title: Issue: Automatic XML Documentation Comments for Exceptions - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

Crawls the call hierarchy for all throws and possible exceptions and automatically compiles the XML comments for exceptions found.

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 7/1/2021, 08:02 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: C# Editor Formatting - Set Spacing For Operators - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

In Tools / Options / Text Editor / C# / Code Style / Formatting / Spacing / Set spacing for operators, there is currently only consideration for Binary operators. Additional consideration is needed for unary operators, specifically in the choice of whether or not to add a space between the variable name and a unary operator.

An example is to set the preference between index++; and index ++;

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 6/29/2021, 07:25 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: C# is auto-completing braces even though settings is: Off - @vsfeedback
item.description:
This issue has been moved from a ticket on Developer Community.

[severity:It bothers me. A fix would be nice] [regression] when I write: if (a == 1) return; else { }

then the IDE will automatically add braces to:

if (a == 1) { return; } else { }

even though I never asked for it. This is new. It happens automatically even though brace-completion is off in VS options. It is unclear which VS option disables this globally because it happens automatically. Maybe you should write something to the output window. Please don't make C# into Visual Basic 6 or PyCharm.

Thanks!

Original Comments

Feedback Bot on 7/1/2021, 11:00 PM:

We have directed your feedback to the appropriate engineering team for further evaluation. The team will review the feedback and notify you about the next steps.

Feedback Bot on 7/4/2021, 07:36 PM:

Thank you for taking the time to provide your suggestion. We will do some preliminary checks to make sure we can proceed further. We'll provide an update once the issue has been triaged by the product team.

item.title: Issue: Intellisense does not work on pointers passed into functions - @ThadHouse
item.description:
Version Used:

Microsoft Visual Studio Enterprise 2019 Version 16.10.2

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Paste the following code in a blank project.
public partial struct NativeThing
{
    public int x;
}

private unsafe static int NativeCallback(NativeThing* handle)
{
    return 0;
}
  1. Inside of NativeCallback, try to access x on handle with handle->

Expected Behavior:

Intellisense for x field shows up

Actual Behavior:

No intellisense at all shows up.

item.title: Issue: F2 Rename `Apply` is inconsistent - @naminodarie
item.description:
Version Used:

  • Microsoft Visual Studio Community 2019 Version 16.10.3
  • Microsoft Visual Studio Community 2022 Preview Version 17.0.0 Preview 2.0

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Create a project like below.
  2. Open Project/RenameFile.csproj.
  3. Focus Program in Source/Program.cs
  4. Press F2 Rename
  5. Check Rename symbol's file
  6. Rename class name
  7. Do
    • Press Enter key
    • Click Apply button

Expected Behavior:

After pressing Enter key or clicking Apply button, an error will be thrown.

Actual Behavior:

After clicking Apply button, an error will be thrown. After pressing Enter key, a line break will be added.

https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/32071278/126186490-660ee62a-4194-412a-a682-5818bd577584.mp4

file tree

.
├── Project
│   └── RenameFile.csproj
└── Source
    └── Program.cs

Project/RenameFile.csproj

<Project Sdk="Microsoft.NET.Sdk">
  <PropertyGroup>
    <OutputType>Library</OutputType>
    <TargetFramework>net5.0</TargetFramework>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <Compile Include="../Source/Program.cs" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

Source/Program.cs

class Program { }

item.title: Issue: Reference failed on parameters in attribute marked on a parameter of primary constructors in records - @SunnieShine
item.description:
Version Used: VS 17.0 Preview 2

Steps to Reproduce:

Here the property Solution has been declared into the current record type. When I mark the MemberNotNullWhenAttribute on a parameter to reference it, it reports "Not found", and we must change the code to be with the type name such as nameof(AnalysisResult.Solution) instead of nameof(Solution) here.

Expected Behavior:

No error.

Actual Behavior:

CS0103 raised.

item.title: Issue: Performance: Basic Keypresses in Solution with All Analyzers Disabled Causes `ServiceHub.RoslynCodeAnalysisService.exe` to Utilize ~10% of CPU - @Mike-E-angelo
item.description:
Version Used: 3.8.0

Steps to Reproduce:

  1. Open this solution
  2. Note all analyzers have been disabled in all projects via <EnableNETAnalyzers>false</EnableNETAnalyzers>
  3. Ensure you are using a custom Refit nupkg which comments out this line and this line, rendering the Refit source generator as a noop
  4. Modify a file with basic key presses (e.g. Enter followed by Backspace)
  5. Observe considerable CPU churn of around 10% even though analyzers have been disabled.

Expected Behavior: When analyzers and/or live analysis are disabled, CPU utilization by ServiceHub.RoslynCodeAnalysisService.exe should be minimal if at all.

Actual Behavior: ServiceHub.RoslynCodeAnalysisService.exe consumes a considerable amount of CPU, even when analyzers are disabled in a solution.

Additional Context: This issue was originally reported here, and then opened as an issue on the Refit repository here.

item.title: Issue: IDE not highlighting class being inherited in VB and marking its namespace as unnecessary using - @Youssef1313
item.description:
Version Used:

16.10.3

Steps to Reproduce:

I'm only hitting this while working with Roslyn.sln.

Also note that removing the namespace fails the build, but the IDE doesn't show the error in error list. It only shows in build output after an explicit build. This also affects other things like add using quick actions, implement abstract class, or codefix tied to a specific compiler error.

item.title: Issue: Offer a codefix for BC40005 - @Youssef1313
item.description:
{0} '{1}' shadows an overridable method in the base {2} '{3}'. To override the base method, this method must be declared 'Overrides'.

I think this error is a candidate to have a codefix associated with it.

NOTE: The codefix should fix access modifiers if needed.

item.title: Issue: Analyzer fix for handling invalid XML in seealso href attributes - @lonix1
item.description:
Brief description:

I have this:

/// <summary>
/// Foo
/// </summary>
/// <seealso href="https://www.example.com/foo?q=bar&qux=2"/>

That gives:

XML comment has badly formed XML -- 'Reference to undefined entity 'qux'.' csharp(CS1570)

The problem is the & is invalid XML.

Workarounds:

  • convert & to &
  • disable the warning with a #pragma warning disable CS1570
  • <NoWarn>1570</NoWarn>
  • find (using regex) and replace & with &

These are non ideal and a waste of time - I just want to paste my URLs into the code and move on.

Could you please consider:

  1. Allowing us to configure the analyzer to allow ampersands
  2. OR BETTER, have an analyzer fix to automatically fix the address as it's pasted it in a seealso href attribute value (like we already have when pasting in a json string -- it doubles \ characters automatically to keep paths valid)

Languages applicable: C#

Code example that the analyzer should report: As above.

Additional information: Originally posted at StackOverflow.

Documentation requirements: When this analyzer is implemented, it must be documented by following the steps at Documentation for IDE CodeStyle analyzers.

item.title: Issue: Auto-generated operations should handle multi-line doc comments correctly - @Youssef1313
item.description:
Version Used:

main

Steps to Reproduce:

This renders as a single line:

Either have the source generator add <para> on its own, or do that in OperationInterfaces.xml

The above is just an example. This may apply to other doc comments
